title: MCP Security, Prompt Injection, and Supply Chain Trust · Skill Brief: Server Trust
summary: Threat-model an MCP server consuming untrusted content and exposing write tools. Planned artifact: Add and verify the agent ext server trust increment in an MCP security test suite and operating policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect server provenance, dependencies, update channel, permissions, and data destinations.
Map scopes and approvals to narrow tools and user intent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

steps.title: Predict Server Trust
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: agent ext mcp security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Inspect server provenance, dependencies, update channel, permissions, and data destinations. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Worked case pattern

steps.title: Defend the Server Trust model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Server Trust applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
